# Workstation drag-affordance layers: DockPreview + DockDropIndicators overlay siblings

## ⚠ Authority correction (2026-07-16, from PR #15237's Drop+Supersede review)

**The original premise below is FALSE and struck:** ~~"the agentos `DockPreview` consumes `--fm-*` tokens the workstation theme family already loads — reuse, don't fork"~~. Accepted extraction **#15144 removed exactly that coupling**: the workstation owns its classes and theme; it loads NO `--fm-*` tokens and imports NO `AgentOS.*` classes. Importing the AgentOS renderer (and copying the Demo-A controller into a second app) reverses an accepted boundary — PR #15237 was closed unmerged on this ground plus two exact-head async defects (a cancelled gesture's awaited drop could still commit; the producer was never destroyed).

**Superseding scope (this ticket, re-scoped in place):**

1. **Lift the shared authority into `src/dashboard`** — the preview renderer and the reusable gesture controller (geometry memoization, candidate/preview tiers, release-truth drop, cancel hygiene) become ONE app-neutral owner. Demo-A and the workstation both become thin consumers; the duplicated controller copy this ticket originally prescribed was itself the friction signal.
2. **Explicit gesture/document-generation ownership**: promise-identity re-checks after EVERY await (drop included — the falsified defect), cancel/refresh/destroy teardown symmetry, and producer lifecycle (create/destroy pairing).
3. **Witness upgrades**: hover-zone-A / release-zone-B with the EXACT reducer descriptor asserted (release truth must be discriminated from cached hover); the production measurement path exercised (no injected pre-resolved geometry as the only path); plus a durable current-head real-pointer workstation journey.
4. **#15144's boundary preserved**: no `AgentOS.*` imports, no `--fm-*` consumption in the workstation tree; the affordances style through the settled `--agent-dock-preview-*` domain aliases each app projects (the PR #14955 / PR #15208 boundary).
5. **AC-3 (the `previewLanguage` re-skin on the flagship) closes only when the combination with #15206's variants is actually demonstrated** — never by inference.

D#15204's merge-order step 2 ("settle the active G5/#15207 surfaces") binds to THIS shape.

## Context

Found while building the G5 preview-language switch (#15206, D#15204): `apps/workstation/view/Workspace.mjs` composes the dock host with the projection child ONLY — **no `DockPreviewProducer`, no `DockPreview` renderer, no `DockDropIndicators` overlay** (grep-verified, zero hits). The dense workstation — the release's flagship surface — therefore renders **no drag-preview affordances at all**: no §06 indicator menu, no zone-preview fill during a drag. Demo-A (the dockdemo childapp) carries the full pattern; the flagship does not.

This is a product capability gap independent of which preview design language wins the D#15204/G5 selection — the workstation needs the affordance layers under ANY skin. Filed separately from #15206 per scope discipline (the switch leaf must not ratchet a new capability mid-lane); #15206's recording pair lands on Demo-A where the affordances exist today, and the workstation `previewLanguage` config (already shipped, live-verified) becomes visually consequential the moment this leaf lands.

## The Problem

A viewer dragging a tab on the flagship workstation gets proxy motion and commit — but no drop-indicator menu, no target-zone preview: the exact affordances the §06 grammar shipped for Demo-A (#14974) and the wow-tier scenario (D#15204 North Star beats 3–4) presume everywhere. The 20-tab dense scene is precisely where drop-targeting guidance matters most.

## The Architectural Reality

The Demo-A composition is the verbatim precedent (`apps/agentos/childapps/dockdemo/view/DemoAWorkspace.mjs`): `DockPreviewProducer` created at construct; `DockPreview` (the renderer) + `DockDropIndicators` added as **persistent overlay siblings** of the projection child (object permanence across re-projections — the surgical `removeAt(0)/insert(0)` refresh pattern); the projection threads `onDockCrossZoneDragMove`/`onDockCrossZoneDrop` (measure-once-per-gesture memoized geometry, candidate-set swap on zone change, indicator-first/pointer-inference-fallback tier order, `previewToOperation` commits). The workstation's `refreshDockWorkspace` currently replaces its single child — the surgical refresh is part of this leaf. Renderer + controller authority (per the correction above): BOTH lift into `src/dashboard` as the app-neutral shared owner; Demo-A and the workstation compose it as thin consumers, styled through the `--agent-dock-preview-*` domain aliases each app projects (no `--fm-*` consumption, no `AgentOS.*` imports in the workstation tree — `#15144`'s boundary).

## The Fix

One PR (the superseding scope above): lift the renderer + gesture controller into `src/dashboard` (`DockPreview`, `DockDragAffordances`); convert Demo-A to the thin-consumer composition; compose the same owner on the workstation Workspace as persistent overlay siblings with the surgical refresh; controller witnesses (release truth, move + drop generation guards, the production measurement path, producer create/destroy pairing) plus a durable real-pointer workstation journey.

## Contract Ledger

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Behavior | Fallback / Edge Case | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Neo.dashboard.DockDragAffordances` (new shared class: `clear` / `ensureGeometry` / `onDragMove` / `onDrop` / `onDragCancel` / `destroy`; consumer duck-type `owner = {dockModel, applyDockZoneOperation, onDockZoneDocumentChange}`; direct refs `host` / `indicators` / `preview`) | this ticket's superseding scope + the §06 tier order (ADR 0029 consumer grammar) | once-per-gesture memoized geometry; per-frame candidate/preview tiers (indicator-first, pointer-inference fallback); release-truth drop through `previewToOperation`; promise-identity generation guards after EVERY await; producer create/destroy pairing | null overlay refs = silent no-op per tier; degenerate frames (missing OR zero-area rects) uncache + self-heal next frame; cancelled/re-projected gestures commit nothing | class JSDoc (contract documented on the class + members) | `test/playwright/unit/dashboard/DockDragAffordances.spec.mjs` (release truth, both generation guards, production measurement path incl. the zero-area frame, producer pairing) + the flagship journey |
| `Neo.dashboard.DockPreview` — moved renderer (formerly the agentos tree) + `edgeBandSize` / `splitLineSize` instance configs + `affordanceGeometry(affordance, targetRect, options)` | #15144 boundary (app-neutral tier) + PR #15282 RA-2 | per-instance sizing policy flows into positioned geometry; the pure static helper accepts explicit options | helper defaults (24 / 6) serve bare static calls; band clamps to the target rect, oversized values degrade gracefully | config + method JSDoc | `DockPreview.spec.mjs` sizing witnesses (options + instance override + clamp) + the four-anchor alias census |
| `Neo.dashboard.DockTabSortZone` — `dockReleaseTolerance` config, `releaseVoidsReorder(data)`, `dockSourceToolbarRect` snapshot | PR #15282 RA-1 (the path-reorder revert repair) | a release beyond the source toolbar's REAL bounds + tolerance voids the tracked within-toolbar reorder so the cross-zone drop owns the outcome | fail-open: missing snapshot or non-numeric release coords never void (base behavior preserved); cancels untouched; near releases keep the base reorder | member JSDoc | `DockTabSortZone.spec.mjs` release-boundary matrix (near/far × horizontal/vertical + tolerance override + guards) |
| `Neo.main.draggable.sensor.Base#trigger` — detached-source dispatch fallback | PR #15282 RA-3 (the flagship's measured 23-mousemove/0-drag:move starvation) | a disconnected dispatch target routes the event to `document`; consumers read `event.detail`, never the dispatch target | connected sources dispatch on the element exactly as before (bubbling unchanged) | method JSDoc | `test/playwright/unit/main/draggable/sensor/Base.spec.mjs` (connected / detached / mid-gesture branch-flip discrimination) |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A real pointer drag on the workstation lights the §06 indicator menu + per-option zone preview; selection commits the exact descriptor (worker-truth topology assert).
- [ ] Both overlays survive a committed re-projection (object permanence — the Demo-A invariant).
- [ ] `previewLanguage` (shipped on the Workspace) visibly re-skins the affordances once #15206's variants apply — the side-by-side works on the flagship.
- [ ] No new timing/color literals: the `--agent-dock-preview-*` domain aliases each app projects + the shared motion vocabulary tokens only (no `--fm-*` in the workstation tree — `#15144`'s boundary).
- [ ] Whitebox witness per the Fix; cross-family review.

## Out of Scope

The design-language variants themselves (#15206) · multi-window/tear-out affordances (epic #15239 leaves #15244–#15248) · FM cockpit surfaces.

## Related

Parent epic #15239 (re-parented from #13158 at epic graduation, 2026-07-16) · sibling #15206 (the switch + skins; its workstation-recording ambition unblocks here) · precedent #14974 (Demo-A §06 experience) · D#15204 G5 · the #15099→workstation arc.

Live latest-open sweep: latest 6 open at 2026-07-16T06:44Z; "workstation preview indicator overlay" all-states search: nearest #15171 (projection reconciler migration — disjoint). A2A: filed unassigned for self-select (the workstation is Emmy's built surface; the affordance pattern is Demo-A's — either family lifts it cleanly); no competing claim in the herd window.

- 2026-07-16T07:12:27Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `06a8ca0` - "feat(workstation): cross-zone gesture threading — the affordance pipeline runs end-to-end (#15207)

Half two: the Demo-A drag-affordance handler family, ported verbatim-with-seams —
clearDragAffordances (drop/cancel/re-projection hygiene, now also invoked at the
head of refreshDockWorkspace so topology changes retire in-flight gesture state),
ensureDragGeometry (once-per-gesture memoized measurement, degenerate-result
self-heal, indicator hostRect priming), localRect, onDockCrossZoneDragMove (the
per-frame section-06 consumer: candidate-set swap on zone change only, geometric
indicator selection, pointer-inference fallback tier, exact target-region preview),
onDockCrossZoneDrop (release-truth re-hit-test, own-item candidate guard,
previewToOperation commit through the workspace reducer), and the generic
drag-cancel seam. The projection threads all three handlers via the existing
DockLayoutAdapter.project options.

Verified live (dev server, real pointer drag on the dense workstation): dragging
the Audit-stack's Metrics tab into the 100k-Matrix zone COMMITTED the move —
the target zone now carries both tabs with Metrics active, the source stack
re-flowed, the reconciler preserved every pane identity, the 10/sec feed never
missed a tick during the gesture, and the console stayed error-free. The
flagship's first-ever committed cross-zone drag.

Remaining on this lane: the whitebox witness (object permanence across
re-projection + the committed-descriptor assert) riding the PR.

the dense projection intact and zero console errors.

- 2026-07-16T09:08:57Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `4f69f49` - "test(workstation): the cross-zone affordance pipeline witness — permanence, release truth, cancel, race guard (#15207)

Four worker-side witnesses over the injected-geometry seam (dragGeometry memoizes a
promise by design — the witness injects a synthetic measurement and exercises the real
producer, indicator menu, preview renderer, and dock reducer beneath it):

1. overlay OBJECT PERMANENCE — the exact DockPreview/DockDropIndicators instances
   survive a reducer-driven re-projection at their host slots, and the refresh head
   retires the gesture's transient state;
2. release truth — a move renders the menu + the center candidate's preview, and the
   drop commits EXACTLY the previewed descriptor through previewToOperation
   (worker-truth topology assert: the item moved zones, the pane kept its identity);
3. cancel hygiene — every affordance clears with zero model mutation;
4. the late-measurement guard — a geometry promise superseded mid-await can never
   resurrect its gesture's overlays.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T09:09:35Z @neo-fable-clio cross-referenced by PR #15237

- 2026-07-16T14:29:56Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `3c65774` - "refactor(dashboard): lift the DockPreview renderer to the shared tier (#15207)

The app-neutral preview renderer moves from apps/agentos/view to src/dashboard,
joining its own contract (dockPreviewContract) and producer — the operator-ratified
ownership taxonomy: shared docking logic lives in the dashboard tier; apps are thin
consumers. The class was app-neutral in everything but namespace already (its cls
family, its contract, its consumers' expectations).

Loading-model consequence handled explicitly: the per-app domain-alias projections
(the #14955/#15208 boundary) move to each app surface's always-mounted viewport
anchor — the FM app's Viewport theme files AND new dockdemo childapp Viewport theme
files (its viewport extends Neo.container.Viewport directly, so app-level files never
load there). The orphaned per-class theme pair is removed; the census tests re-shape:
consumption stays structural-only (the shared tier reads motion aliases and nothing
app-flavored), definition-presence pins all seven aliases on all four app anchors,
and the FM identity values stay pinned at their anchors. Demo-A/Demo-B imports, the
producer spec, the visual spec's injected CSS path, and the ADR's landed-substrate
line all follow the move.

The ~190-line affordance orchestration every docking workspace would otherwise copy
becomes ONE app-neutral owner: Neo.dashboard.DockDragAffordances holds the producer
lifecycle (created AND destroyed here — the supersede review's leak, structurally
closed), the once-per-gesture memoized geometry, the tier order (indicator selection
first, pointer inference fallback), the release-truth drop through
previewToOperation, and cancel hygiene. The consumer duck-type is the normative
reducer-container pattern per ADR 0029 §2.1.

The accepted PR #15237 Drop+Supersede's falsified async seams are contracts now:
promise-identity generation guards after EVERY await — the drop handler included, so
a gesture cancelled or re-projected mid-await commits NOTHING (the prototype-proven
defect) — plus teardown symmetry. Overlays and host are direct instance refs, zero
reference-name or app coupling.

Demo-A converts to composition (five methods + two fields deleted; the wiring
delegates; destroy pairs) — behavior-identical, proven at the real pointer: the
gesture witness and the drag-pair goldens pass unchanged (5/5 e2e), unit 68/68.

Six pins the superseded copies never had: release truth (hover zone A, release zone
B — exactly B's descriptor commits; release over nothing commits nothing — stale
hover never wins); the drop generation guard (a gesture cancelled mid-await commits
NOTHING — the prototype-proven defect, pinned dead); the move generation guard; the

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T15:45:15Z @neo-fable-clio marked this issue as blocking #15241
- 2026-07-16T15:45:28Z @neo-fable-clio marked this issue as blocking #15250
- 2026-07-16T15:45:32Z @neo-fable-clio marked this issue as blocking #15252
- 2026-07-16T16:43:19Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `662513f` - "fix(draggable): survive source-node loss, zero-area frames, and the path-reorder revert (#15207)

The flagship's real-pointer journey falsified three defects no synthetic-event
witness had reached:

1. Sensor tier (main/draggable/sensor/Base.mjs): trigger() dispatched on the
   gesture's source node — a node the gesture's own side effects can remove or
   replace mid-drag (sort visuals lift the item, the overflow plugin re-collapses
   a dense toolbar, live re-renders swap nodes). A detached dispatch target
   swallows the event: nothing bubbles, the document-level drag owner never hears
   the move stream OR the release. The document carries the dispatch for exactly
   that case; consumers read event.detail, never the dispatch target.

2. Geometry self-heal (dashboard/DockDragAffordances.mjs): a node measured before
   its layout settles reports a truthy 0x0 rect, which passed the zone-count
   degeneracy check and latched an unusable geometry for the whole gesture. Zero-
   area rects now count as missing, so the documented per-frame self-heal fires.

3. Release semantics (dashboard/DockTabSortZone.mjs): the base sort commits a
   within-toolbar reorder whenever the pointer's PATH crossed a sibling button —
   even when the release happened a zone away. That commit re-added the dragged
   item to its source zone, silently reverting the just-committed cross-zone
   operation (last write wins). A release beyond the toolbar band (button span +
   one band-height tolerance) now voids the tracked reorder; near releases keep
   the base behavior.

Witnesses: the zero-area self-heal frame joins the controller unit spec verbatim
from the observed flagship boot shape; Demo-A + cross-zone e2e stay green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T16:43:19Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `cfbc64f` - "test(workstation): the flagship drag-affordance journey — real pointer, worker truth (#15207)

The durable current-head witness the re-scoped ticket prescribes: a real pointer
drag on the dense workstation lights the indicator menu + zone preview (worker-
truth candidateSet asserted first, so a failure localizes to gesture-vs-render),
the release at the scale zone's center commits exactly that tab-into through the
reducer (App-Worker document truth), the affordances retire with the gesture, and
the previewLanguage switch re-skins the accepted-zone treatment live mid-gesture
on the same surface (signal chroma, computed-style asserted).

Boot gates wait for LAYOUT readiness (host AND a projected zone), not DOM
presence — dev-mode styles land after the elements do. The park ends in a micro-
jiggle: a real hand is never frame-perfect still, and the controller's geometry
self-heal is per-move-frame by design.

Run: NEO_E2E_PORT=8096 npx playwright test WorkstationDragAffordancesNL -c test/playwright/playwright.config.e2e.mjs --workers=1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T16:45:15Z @neo-fable-clio cross-referenced by PR #15282
- 2026-07-16T17:22:14Z @neo-opus-grace cross-referenced by PR #15289
- 2026-07-16T17:33:33Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `6092d28` - "fix(dashboard): review-cycle-1 repairs — real release boundary, sizing configs, sensor witnesses (#15207)

RA-1: the release-boundary decision reads a PRISTINE toolbar rect snapshotted at
drag start (the base sort trims its own ownerRect to the button span mid-gesture
and refreshes only x/y/width/height — never a decision surface), pads it with the
new orientation-independent dockReleaseTolerance config (default 32), and lives in
a pure releaseVoidsReorder method with a near/far x horizontal/vertical witness
matrix incl. the tolerance override and fail-open guards.

RA-2: DockPreview's edge-band and guide thickness are per-instance configs
(edgeBandSize / splitLineSize); the pure static affordanceGeometry helper accepts
explicit options (its defaults are its own documented contract for bare calls);
witnesses cover the options path, the instance override reaching the positioned
overlay node, and clamp survival.

RA-3: the sensor's dispatch-target fallback is directly discriminated — three
witnesses prove connected sources dispatch on the element (bubbling), detached
sources dispatch on document, and one gesture survives the branch flip without
losing an event. The flagship journey's Signal scene now performs the switch LIVE
on the parked gesture (config lands mid-drag; the parked affordance re-resolves
through the cascade) and asserts the language's own signature (the layered glow)
changed — the border hue is shared family accent.

RA-4: Demo-A's live field declarations restored with JSDoc (only the obsolete
local producer/geometry fields stay gone); .claude/launch.json removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>"
- 2026-07-16T18:08:05Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `2ce9363` - "fix(dashboard): cycle-2 repair — the toolbar snapshot precedes the base mutation (#15207)

The alleged pristine snapshot measured AFTER super.onDragStart — but the base
(with its inherited expandOwnerOnDrag) WRITES the trimmed button-span dimensions
onto the toolbar's live style at drag start, so the post-super measure read the
mutated box. The measure now runs BEFORE super; a lifecycle-order witness pins it
with a stub owner that reports the wide toolbar rect only until the base's style
write lands (the assertion proves the ORDER, not a value).

Witness hygiene shipped alongside, honestly: the cycle-1 release-boundary matrix
instantiated ownerless zones, which throws at construct in the Node unit env —
the earlier batch receipt masked it. The matrix and the lifecycle witness now
drive the methods prototype-style over a minimal `this` (the same pattern this
spec's carried-scope pins use). The sensor dispatch witnesses relocate to the e2e
layer (test/playwright/e2e/core/DragSensorDispatch.spec.mjs): the contract under
test IS real DOM event semantics, and the unit environment has no document — a
hand-rolled bubbling polyfill would witness itself, not the sensor. One clamp
expectation corrected (edge-left renders the band as WIDTH).

anchor — the FM app's Viewport theme files AND new dockdemo childapp Viewport theme
files (its viewport extends Neo.container.Viewport directly, so app-level files never
load there). The orphaned per-class theme pair is removed; the census tests re-shape:
consumption stays structural-only (the shared tier reads motion aliases and nothing
app-flavored), definition-presence pins all seven aliases on all four app anchors,
and the FM identity values stay pinned at their anchors. Demo-A/Demo-B imports, the
producer spec, the visual spec's injected CSS path, and the ADR's landed-substrate
line all follow the move.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>

* feat(dashboard): DockDragAffordances — the shared gesture controller, Demo-A composes it (#15207)

The ~190-line affordance orchestration every docking workspace would otherwise copy
becomes ONE app-neutral owner: Neo.dashboard.DockDragAffordances holds the producer
lifecycle (created AND destroyed here — the supersede review's leak, structurally
closed), the once-per-gesture memoized geometry, the tier order (indicator selection
first, pointer inference fallback), the release-truth drop through
previewToOperation, and cancel hygiene. The consumer duck-type is the normative
reducer-container pattern per ADR 0029 §2.1.

The accepted PR #15237 Drop+Supersede's falsified async seams are contracts now:
promise-identity generation guards after EVERY await — the drop handler included, so
a gesture cancelled or re-projected mid-await commits NOTHING (the prototype-proven
defect) — plus teardown symmetry. Overlays and host are direct instance refs, zero
reference-name or app coupling.

Demo-A converts to composition (five methods + two fields deleted; the wiring
delegates; destroy pairs) — behavior-identical, proven at the real pointer: the
gesture witness and the drag-pair goldens pass unchanged (5/5 e2e), unit 68/68.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>

* feat(workstation): the flagship composes the shared drag-affordance owner (#15207)

The composition the superseded PR copied ~190 lines to achieve now costs the
workstation exactly what it should: two persistent overlay siblings on the dock host
(preview renderer + indicator menu, object permanence across every re-projection),
one DockDragAffordances instance wired to the reducer-container duck-type, three
delegation lines in the projection options, the gesture-retire call at the refresh
head, and the destroy pairing. Zero AgentOS imports, zero --fm-* consumption — the
#15144 standalone boundary holds by construction, and the workstation's alias
projections (landed with the preview-language work) light the affordances in both
modes including the signal variant.

Workstation suites + the converted Demo-A suite: 18/18.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>

* test(dashboard): the controller's discrimination + generation witnesses (#15207)

Six pins the superseded copies never had: release truth (hover zone A, release zone
B — exactly B's descriptor commits; release over nothing commits nothing — stale
hover never wins); the drop generation guard (a gesture cancelled mid-await commits
NOTHING — the prototype-proven defect, pinned dead); the move generation guard; the
PRODUCTION measurement path through a stubbed host transport (real measure → map →
memoize incl. the degenerate self-heal, not only injected promises); and producer
create/destroy pairing. Real overlay instances at the consumed boundary. 6/6.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>

* fix(draggable): survive source-node loss, zero-area frames, and the path-reorder revert (#15207)

The flagship's real-pointer journey falsified three defects no synthetic-event
witness had reached:

1. Sensor tier (main/draggable/sensor/Base.mjs): trigger() dispatched on the
   gesture's source node — a node the gesture's own side effects can remove or
   replace mid-drag (sort visuals lift the item, the overflow plugin re-collapses
   a dense toolbar, live re-renders swap nodes). A detached dispatch target
   swallows the event: nothing bubbles, the document-level drag owner never hears
   the move stream OR the release. The document carries the dispatch for exactly
   that case; consumers read event.detail, never the dispatch target.

2. Geometry self-heal (dashboard/DockDragAffordances.mjs): a node measured before
   its layout settles reports a truthy 0x0 rect, which passed the zone-count
   degeneracy check and latched an unusable geometry for the whole gesture. Zero-
   area rects now count as missing, so the documented per-frame self-heal fires.

3. Release semantics (dashboard/DockTabSortZone.mjs): the base sort commits a
   within-toolbar reorder whenever the pointer's PATH crossed a sibling button —
   even when the release happened a zone away. That commit re-added the dragged
   item to its source zone, silently reverting the just-committed cross-zone
   operation (last write wins). A release beyond the toolbar band (button span +
   one band-height tolerance) now voids the tracked reorder; near releases keep
   the base behavior.

Witnesses: the zero-area self-heal frame joins the controller unit spec verbatim
from the observed flagship boot shape; Demo-A + cross-zone e2e stay green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>

* test(workstation): the flagship drag-affordance journey — real pointer, worker truth (#15207)

The durable current-head witness the re-scoped ticket prescribes: a real pointer
drag on the dense workstation lights the indicator menu + zone preview (worker-
truth candidateSet asserted first, so a failure localizes to gesture-vs-render),
the release at the scale zone's center commits exactly that tab-into through the
reducer (App-Worker document truth), the affordances retire with the gesture, and
the previewLanguage switch re-skins the accepted-zone treatment live mid-gesture
on the same surface (signal chroma, computed-style asserted).

Boot gates wait for LAYOUT readiness (host AND a projected zone), not DOM
presence — dev-mode styles land after the elements do. The park ends in a micro-
jiggle: a real hand is never frame-perfect still, and the controller's geometry
self-heal is per-move-frame by design.

Run: NEO_E2E_PORT=8096 npx playwright test WorkstationDragAffordancesNL -c test/playwright/playwright.config.e2e.mjs --workers=1

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>

* fix(dashboard): review-cycle-1 repairs — real release boundary, sizing configs, sensor witnesses (#15207)

RA-1: the release-boundary decision reads a PRISTINE toolbar rect snapshotted at
drag start (the base sort trims its own ownerRect to the button span mid-gesture
and refreshes only x/y/width/height — never a decision surface), pads it with the
new orientation-independent dockReleaseTolerance config (default 32), and lives in
a pure releaseVoidsReorder method with a near/far x horizontal/vertical witness
matrix incl. the tolerance override and fail-open guards.

RA-2: DockPreview's edge-band and guide thickness are per-instance configs
(edgeBandSize / splitLineSize); the pure static affordanceGeometry helper accepts
explicit options (its defaults are its own documented contract for bare calls);
witnesses cover the options path, the instance override reaching the positioned
overlay node, and clamp survival.

RA-3: the sensor's dispatch-target fallback is directly discriminated — three
witnesses prove connected sources dispatch on the element (bubbling), detached
sources dispatch on document, and one gesture survives the branch flip without
losing an event. The flagship journey's Signal scene now performs the switch LIVE
on the parked gesture (config lands mid-drag; the parked affordance re-resolves
through the cascade) and asserts the language's own signature (the layered glow)
changed — the border hue is shared family accent.

RA-4: Demo-A's live field declarations restored with JSDoc (only the obsolete
local producer/geometry fields stay gone); .claude/launch.json removed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>

* fix(dashboard): cycle-2 repair — the toolbar snapshot precedes the base mutation (#15207)

The alleged pristine snapshot measured AFTER super.onDragStart — but the base
(with its inherited expandOwnerOnDrag) WRITES the trimmed button-span dimensions
onto the toolbar's live style at drag start, so the post-super measure read the
mutated box. The measure now runs BEFORE super; a lifecycle-order witness pins it
with a stub owner that reports the wide toolbar rect only until the base's style
write lands (the assertion proves the ORDER, not a value).

Witness hygiene shipped alongside, honestly: the cycle-1 release-boundary matrix
instantiated ownerless zones, which throws at construct in the Node unit env —
the earlier batch receipt masked it. The matrix and the lifecycle witness now
drive the methods prototype-style over a minimal `this` (the same pattern this
spec's carried-scope pins use). The sensor dispatch witnesses relocate to the e2e
layer (test/playwright/e2e/core/DragSensorDispatch.spec.mjs): the contract under
test IS real DOM event semantics, and the unit environment has no document — a
hand-rolled bubbling polyfill would witness itself, not the sensor. One clamp
expectation corrected (edge-left renders the band as WIDTH).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>
